---
title: "Customer Retention Cohort Analysis"
date: "2025-11-17"
categories: [Machine Learning, Portofolio]
description: Understanding user behavior over time through cohort visualization
format:
  html:
    echo: fenced  
    code-fold: false
    notebook-links: false
---

Dataset: 
- "https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/gsagar12/dspp1?resource=download&select=customer_product.csv"
-   https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/gsagar12/dspp1?resource=download

---

## Objective
Answer the key business questions:  
- How long do customers typically stay active?  
- When do most users start dropping off?  
- Which acquisition cohorts perform the best?

## Methodology
- Cohorts defined by **first transaction month**
- Retention measured as percentage of customers from a cohort who remain active in subsequent periods  
- Activity = at least one transaction in the period  
- Heatmap + line chart visualization

## Key Findings
- Average **30-day retention**: 38 %  
- Average **90-day retention**: 18 %  
- Best cohort (Jan 2024): 52 % still active after 6 months  
- Worst cohort (Aug 2024): only 11 % active after 3 months (possible promo-driven users)

![Retention Heatmap (Monthly Cohorts)](images/cohort_heatmap.png)
![Retention Curves by Cohort](images/cohort_curves.png)

## Business Implications
| Insight                              | Recommended Action                          |
|--------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Sharp drop after month 1             | Improve onboarding & first-use experience   |
| Jan–Mar cohorts significantly better | Replicate acquisition channels from Q1      |
| High churn in promo-heavy cohorts    | Focus on quality over quantity of new users |

**Tech Stack:** 
- Python 
- Pandas 
- Seaborn 
- Matplotlib
